_comment: What the /wrap Claude skill produces after reading a working session. Extraction is the skill's job; this is its output, so the fixture stands in for the model rather than for the session. Four items of three kinds, which is what makes the anchor worth watching: the decision goes to Notion, the task to TickTick, and neither moves until Sandip says so.

items.title: Local embeddings by default, cloud only on explicit opt-in
items.detail: Recall runs all-MiniLM-L6-v2 in-process. A cloud embedding provider stays available but never becomes the silent default, so no passage of the vault leaves the machine without a config change made on purpose.
items.evidence: TECH-SPEC §7 and §9 both call for it; the config block defaults synthesis to none.

items.title: Every test passed whether or not the vector extension loaded
items.detail: sqlite-vec silently failed to load for two separate reasons and the suite stayed green both times, because the JS fallback returns the same answers more slowly. A correct result is not evidence that the intended path ran.
items.evidence: Found by adding a test that asserts the extension is loaded, not that search works.

items.title: Restart the daemon so migration 5 reaches the live store
items.detail: The running daemon is on migration 4. Recall's tables exist only in test databases until it restarts.

items.title: The Notion adapter ignores idempotency_key
items.detail: §10 requires adapters to check-or-create by key. The Notion one does not, so a re-ingested settled item would create a second row rather than replaying the first.
